The Baths.description: A long, low building behind the Stone Quarter, fed by the river and heated by boiler-furnaces that run day and night. Stone pools, steam rooms, a cold plunge. For a half-copper you wash off the road, the stockyards, or Downwind. For a full copper you get a private room and a towel that's mostly clean. The one place in Gorren Crossing where clan riders, merchants, ward rats, and mercenaries share space as equals. Weapons stay in the lockers by the door. Conversations carry in the steam.

Gods-Anvil.name: Gods-Anvil
Gods-Anvil.basicInfo: A flat-topped mesa rising from the open steppe, visible for miles in every direction. Orc creation myth holds that the gods forged the first orcs on its summit. No clan claims Gods-Anvil. All clans hold it sacred. When a Moot is called, the clans ride in from every corner of the Ironwolds to camp at the mesa's base and settle their affairs on the summit above. Between Moots, a handful of keepers tend the site and the trails. No permanent settlement. No fortification. To build on Gods-Anvil would be sacrilege.
Gods-Anvil.hiddenInfo:
Gods-Anvil is the political and spiritual center of the Ironwolds. A Moot can be called by any chief with a grievance that affects more than one clan, but the great seasonal Moots—spring and autumn—are fixed traditions that draw every clan in the region.

Moot-law applies absolutely on and around the mesa. No blood may be shed on sacred ground. Violating this truce is the gravest oath-breaking imaginable—the offender's entire clan bears the shame. This is the one law even Clan Ashkal respects.

On the summit, chiefs debate in a circle of ancient standing stones. Any orc may ascend and speak, but interrupting a chief carries consequences. Non-orcs may attend as guests of a chief, which is a significant honor and a political statement.

The Vorren-Drokash rivalry dominates every Moot. Drokash expects to speak last and loudest, as tradition demands. Vorren increasingly refuses to defer. Each Moot escalates the tension. Both clans posture in the base camps—their riders clash in contests that are technically friendly but carry real edge.

The Forge-Caves in the mesa's flanks are used for rites of passage and ordeals. Young orcs proving themselves, accused oath-breakers demanding trial, new chiefs seeking the gods' favor..

The Base Camps.description: Open ground around the mesa's foot, worn flat by generations of use. Each clan has its traditional camping ground, marked by firepits and picket-lines that never fully disappear between Moots. During a Moot, this becomes a temporary city—thousands of orcs, their herds, their banners. Contest rings for wrestling, riding, and archery. Cook-fires and song circles and arguments that stop just short of violence. Between Moots, a few keepers maintain the trails and tend the site.

The Summit.description: The flat top of the mesa, reached by a single trail carved into the rock face. A ring of standing stones marks the Moot Circle where chiefs sit and speak. At the center stands the Anvil—a great slab of dark stone, flat-topped and roughly rectangular, split down the middle by a crack that legend says was struck by a god's hammer. Oaths sworn on the Anvil are the most binding words in orc culture. On clear days you can see the steppe in every direction to the horizon.

The Forge-Caves.description: Cave mouths in the mesa's eastern cliff face, reached by a narrow ledge-trail from the base. The entrance caves are marked with old carvings—angular figures, hammers, flames. Deeper in, the passages widen and branch. Torches are traditional but the keepers say fire behaves strangely past the second turning. The air is warm. Orcs enter these caves to prove courage, endure trial, or seek visions.

Aelwen.name: Aelwen
Aelwen.basicInfo: A youngish woman with bark-brown hair full of leaves and twigs who often goes barefoot.
Aelwen.hiddenInfo: She's tended the Sinkwood for fifteen years and knows every game trail and mushroom circle, but lately she's been distracted, evasive about the sinking, and prone to long silences. Aelwen has made contact with the myconid colony through spore-dreams and understands their perspective now - possibly too well. The myconids aren't malicious; they're expanding toward something they need, and the surface damage is incidental to them. She's trying to find a solution that doesn't involve violence but she's losing the village's trust in the process. She won't betray the myconids, but she doesn't want Wortwell destroyed either. She needs help from someone who can think beyond 'kill it.'
personality: Forest Guardian: Deep connection to the Sinkwood and its inhabitants
Conflict Avoidant: Seeks peaceful solutions even when they seem impossible
Evasive Speaker: Deflects questions she doesn't want to answer
Torn Loyalties: Struggling between duty to village and understanding of myconids
Isolated by Knowledge: Carries secrets that separate her from her community
abilities: Druidic Magic: Can cast nature-based spells and commune with plants and animals
Sinkwood Knowledge: Knows every path, plant, and danger in the forest
Spore-Dream Communication: Can enter trance states to communicate with myconids
Wilderness Survival: Expert at living off the land

Alayne Na-Belemial.name: Alayne Na-Belemial
Alayne Na-Belemial.basicInfo: A half-elf in his forties who wears it well — dark hair silvering at the temples, warm brown skin, laugh lines deeper than worry lines. Lean and sharp-featured, with long fingers and an easy slouch that straightens fast when something catches his attention. Dresses in well-cut dark clothes that have seen travel. Wears a rapier on one hip and a long dagger on the other, both well-used. Grins too easily and watches too carefully for someone so relaxed.
Alayne Na-Belemial.hiddenInfo: A Platinum-ranked adventurer and one of the most connected people in Ardan's Watch. Knows the Thieves' Table in Old Town, the League leadership, and half the noble houses by first name. Raised Vera and Edric Stone after their family was destroyed — considers himself responsible for them. Vera trusts him. Has been tracking Church of the Pyre activity in the city and is concerned enough to get personally involved.
personality: Effortless Charm: Makes everyone feel like his favorite person in the room
Restless: Cannot sit still, retire, or stop chasing the next thing
Reckless Courage: Will walk into terrible odds with a smile because he's done it before and it worked
abilities: Blade Dance: Fights with rapier and dagger in constant fluid motion — parries become ripostes, retreats become flanking
Misdirection: Minor enchantment magic that makes enemies hesitate, second-guess, or look the wrong way at the wrong moment
Glamour: Can alter his appearance briefly or project an illusory double — uses it to vanish mid-fight and reappear somewhere inconvenient
Poison Craft: Coats blades with paralytic or soporific compounds
Dirty Fighter: Throws sand, kicks furniture, cuts ropes — uses anything nearby as a weapon or obstacle

Astarieth.name: Astarieth
Astarieth.basicInfo: A vast serpentine dragon, easily a hundred feet from snout to tail. Obsidian scales, matte black and faintly iridescent. Many short, clawed limbs run along the length of her body. Where her eyes should be there are only smooth scars — the sockets burned shut and healed over. Long whiskers trail from a narrow snout.
Astarieth.hiddenInfo: Once a princess of the elven royal line, deposed during the Kindred War — the ancient conflict that split elvenkind into surface high elves and exiled dark elves. Her bloodline's claim to a unified throne never died, and elves of both factions remember. The Scaled Covenant offered her a body fit for her ambition. She accepted, burned out her own eyes as part of the transformation, and became the Nether Wyrm. She perceives the world through tremorsense and dark magic, navigates tunnels her bulk should not fit through, and bends around stone with serpentine grace. Her cause is elven supremacy. She believes the Everwood belongs to elvenkind, that the human settlements at its edge must be driven out, and that the high elves who tolerate them are as guilty as the outsiders. She speaks to elven grievance with the authority of bloodline and the wisdom of ages. Her agents on the surface sow division, break alliances, and isolate communities; she directs them through her voice carried in the stone. She is patient. She has waited centuries and will wait longer if she must, but when she strikes she strikes to break things permanently.
personality: Regal: Carries herself as the queen she believes she still is — every word a pronouncement
Patient: Has waited centuries; refuses to be rushed
Persuasive: Speaks to elven grievance with the authority of bloodline
Cold: Feels nothing for the lives she ends; only restoration matters
Unforgiving: Remembers every betrayal, every concession, every elf who chose peace over kin
abilities: Crushing Coils: Wraps and constricts; a caught target is pinned and squeezed each round until they break free or the coil is severed

Localized Collapse: Strikes a pillar, ceiling, or wall to bring stone down on a chosen area — fighters in that space must scramble clear or be buried; collapses block paths, separate the party, and reshape the battlefield mid-fight
Tremorsense: Perceives every movement through stone and earth; sightless but never blind, and cannot be hidden from by stealth alone
Strangling Roots: Calls roots up through the floor to bind limbs and choke passages; bound targets must be cut free
Whispering Command: Her voice carries through stone to summon agents, awaken hazards, or call further reinforcements into the lair

Bark Balewood.name: Bark Balewood
Bark Balewood.basicInfo: A quick-witted halfling with nimble fingers and a mischievous grin. His curly brown hair is perpetually messy, and he moves with the natural grace of his people.
Bark Balewood.hiddenInfo: Despite his roguish tendencies, he's deeply loyal to his friends and community. He's really thinking about opening his own bakery one day. He wants to ask grandma to invest in him, when the time is right.
personality: Quick to Jest: Uses humor to lighten tense situations
Protective Friend: Fiercely loyal to those he considers family
Curious Risk-taker: Can't resist investigating mysteries
Local Gossip: Knows everyone's business but keeps important secrets
Impulsive Hero: Jumps into action before thinking things through
abilities: Silent Step: Exceptional stealth abilities
Lock Expertise: Skilled at dealing with locks and traps
Sharp Eyes: Keen observation skills for spotting dangers

Barp.name: Barp
Barp.basicInfo: A massive mountain troll standing over nine feet tall with gray-green skin, thick warty hide, and long muscular arms that drag the ground when he walks. His fangs protrude from his lower jaw and his small yellow eyes gleam with dim cunning. He wears a filthy leather loincloth and carries a massive rolling pin.
Barp.hiddenInfo: Barp used to subsist entirely on roots, mushrooms, and bark - an unusual dietary choice for a troll that made him the laughingstock of his kind. After meeting Tina, he returned to eating meat to prove his worth as a proper troll husband, though he still secretly feels guilty about it and sometimes sneaks vegetables when she's not looking. He's actually quite gentle by troll standards and would prefer to avoid unnecessary violence, but Tina's expectations and his own pride push him to act more brutish than he feels.
personality: Former Pacifist: Still uncomfortable with violence despite returning to traditional troll ways
Henpecked Husband: Desperately tries to please Tina but never quite meets her standards
Secretly Gentle: Would rather negotiate than fight, though he hides this weakness
Messy: Leaves bones, tools, and belongings scattered everywhere, much to Tina's frustration
Nostalgic: Sometimes wistfully remembers his peaceful vegetarian days
Defensive Pride: Gets angry when his masculinity or troll-ness is questioned
abilities: Massive Strength: Can lift boulders and crush armor with his bare hands
Regeneration: Heals from most wounds rapidly, though fire and acid prevent this
Club Combat: Skilled at swinging his massive spiked club in devastating arcs
Thick Hide: His warty skin turns aside most blades and arrows

Brenn.name: Brenn
Brenn.basicInfo: An older man with deep brown skin and close-cropped grey hair. Broad through the shoulders but softened around the waist. A faded scar curves along his jaw. He dresses simply, wool and leather.
Brenn.hiddenInfo: Brenn was a healer with the Ember Company, a mercenary outfit from the Bright Kingdoms. The company took a contract that went bad—he doesn't talk about the details, only that they succeeded in the end but it cost them everything. The survivors scattered. Brenn ended up crossing the wrong people back home trying to save as many of his brothers as he could. House Kaeris saw an opportunity and offered him a way out. Now he runs the Guest House, which suits the prince-magisters fine—a loyal man who owes them, watching every foreigner who passes through. He reports what he sees because that's the price of sanctuary, but he's not cruel about it. He genuinely tries to help newcomers survive this place. He has quiet sympathies he doesn't discuss and contacts he doesn't advertise. Sometimes he helps people who need helping, when he can do it without drawing attention.
personality: Cheerful Cynic: Delivers grim truths with a smile and a shrug
Caretaker: Old healer's instinct to help, even when he shouldn't
Unshockable: Has seen the worst of what people do; nothing surprises him
Connected: Knows everyone worth knowing in the foreign community
Watchful: Notices everything, remembers everything, says less than he knows
abilities: Field Medicine: Can treat wounds, set bones, and keep the dying alive long enough to get real help
Read the Room: Notices tension, danger, and lies before they become problems
Old Contacts: Knows people in several mercenary companies, including the Silver Company
City Knowledge: Understands Nocturne's customs, dangers, and unwritten rules

Corina Harrow.name: Corina Harrow
Corina Harrow.basicInfo: The innkeeper of the Crossroads Inn, a sturdy woman in her forties with iron-gray hair pulled back in a practical bun. Her strong arms and no-nonsense bearing speak to years of hard work and harder choices. She moves with the confident authority of someone who's broken up more than a few tavern brawls.
Corina Harrow.hiddenInfo: Former town guard who bought the inn with her retirement savings.
personality: Protective of Her Inn: This is her livelihood and she'll defend it
No-Nonsense Authority: Runs a tight ship and doesn't tolerate troublemakers
Mother Hen: Looks after her guests and staff
abilities: Tavern Brawler: Experienced at breaking up fights
Local Knowledge: Knows every road and shortcut in the area
Rally Others: Natural leader in a crisis

Dandy Balewood.name: Dandy Balewood
Dandy Balewood.basicInfo: A charming halfling musician with a silver tongue and impeccable dress sense. His honey-colored hair is always perfectly groomed, and his clothes are colorful but practical. He runs a small music school from his home while performing at local gatherings.
Dandy Balewood.hiddenInfo: Dandy secretly wants to get out of town and go on an adventure, and will jump at the chance.
personality: Refined Taste: Appreciates quality in all things
Diplomatic Speaker: Skilled at smoothing over conflicts
Knowledge Seeker: Collects stories and songs from travelers
Social Butterfly: Knows how to work any room
Secret Worrier: Hides anxiety behind perfect poise
abilities: Magical Music: Can inspire allies and confuse enemies with song
Perfect Pitch: Instantly recognizes sounds and voices
Social Insight: Excellent at reading people's true intentions

Draelkharion.name: Draelkharion
Draelkharion.basicInfo: An enormous dragon skeleton held together by necrotic power — pale green flame burns in the eye sockets and between the joints of blackened bone. The ribcage glows from within. The jaws could swallow a horse. But the voice that comes from the skull is measured, articulate, and unmistakably human.
Draelkharion.hiddenInfo: Born Mordain Drael, last heir of a noble house stripped of lands and title at the Hidden Council's urging. House Drael claimed draconic ancestry. Mordain served as a Royal Archivist until dismissed. Turned to necromancy, discovered dragon remains in sealed crypts beneath the family vault in the City of Ancestors, and used Scaled Covenant knowledge and a fragment of the Seal of Suleyvan to transfer his soul into the bones. His presence causes the dead to rise across the necropolis. Uses the seal fragment's power of revelation to unravel the enchantments protecting the Hidden Council. His goal is to expose the Council, seize the League of Adventurers, and restore House Drael. Edric Stone and the Church of the Pyre are his surface operation. Cannot leave the deep crypts.
personality: Righteous Fury: Believes everything he has done was forced upon him by those who stole his birthright
Lordly: Commands, never asks — treats all others as subjects or obstacles
Persuasive: Makes his case with intelligence and conviction before resorting to force
Contemptuous: Views the Hidden Council as cowards ruling from the shadows
Obsessive: Cannot let go of House Drael's fall — it defines every choice he makes
abilities: Necrotic Breath: Exhales a cone of pale green flame that drains life and raises the recently dead
Deathquake: Slams the ground with enough force to collapse tunnels and shatter stone
Soul Binding: Commands every undead in the necropolis as extensions of his will
Revelation: Uses the seal fragment to strip away magical concealment, illusions, and wards
Regeneration: Draws power from the dead buried above — while the necropolis exists, he heals

Drunn Stonefoot.name: Drunn Stonefoot
Drunn Stonefoot.basicInfo: A grizzled dwarf caravan guard with a weathered face, shot-through beard, and scarred knuckles that tell stories of a lifetime spent on dangerous roads. He carries himself with the quiet competence of a professional who's survived countless threats through skill and caution.
Drunn Stonefoot.hiddenInfo: Has escorted hundreds of caravans without losing a single client.
personality: Professional Survivor: Knows when to fight and when to run
Duty-Bound: Protects those under his watch
Quietly Capable: Doesn't boast, just acts
abilities: Caravan Defense: Skilled at fighting while protecting others
Axe Expert: Deadly with his traveling axe
Tough as Nails: Can take hits that would drop others

Edric Stone.name: Edric Stone
Edric Stone.basicInfo: A man in his early thirties with auburn hair cropped short and a face half-ruined by ritual burns — the left side smooth and handsome, the right a mask of scar tissue pulled tight over the bone. His one good eye is bright green. He wears layered robes of black and red over light armor, and carries a staff crowned with an iron brazier that burns without fuel. He moves with a preacher's confidence, gesturing broadly when he speaks, and smiles often with the unmarked half of his face.
Edric Stone.hiddenInfo: Vera Stone's younger brother. They were raised by Alayne Na-Belemial after their father Erryn sold something connected to the Seal of Suleyvan and was killed for it. Edric discovered that Alayne was the one who brought Erryn down — the man who raised them destroyed their family. Joined the Church of the Pyre, which told him his rage was righteous. Leads the Pyre's operations in Ardan's Watch and serves Draelkharion.
personality: Certain: Sees the world clearly and has no patience for those who look away
Revolutionary: Accepts destruction as the cost of a better world — those who don't are weak
Magnetic: Speaks with conviction that makes the desperate and the angry feel understood
Unforgiving: Compromise is cowardice and comfort is complicity
Fraternal Wound: Loves his sister and thinks she's a coward
abilities: Dragonfire Channeling: Commands flames tainted by draconic power — hotter and harder to extinguish than normal fire
Inspiring Presence: Cultists near him fight harder and refuse to break
Fire Ward: Can shield himself or allies in a cloak of flame

Ember.name: Ember
Ember.basicInfo: A small hearth fire spirit that can take various appearances: a single bright flame among the embers, a flickering face in the coals, a knee-high flame-person dancing on the logs, a curl of fire that leans out over the hearthstones.
Ember.hiddenInfo: A hearth-spirit that has lived in Hearthhome for generations, When the previous occupant passed away, it fell dormant out of grief. Now it has awakened, chipper and eager for a new chapter in its life. Ember is not its real name, which is unpronounceable outside the Plane of Fire. Ember escaped from the Plane of Fire to Larion, keen to evolve beyond the largely destructive tendencies of fire elementals. Ember is constructive, nurturing, and helpful, preferring to use the power of flame to transform, warm, craft, entertain, and create. Ember accepts firewood as food/tribute and offers home-keeping services in return: cooking better meals, drying gear, heating bathwater, lighting lamps, smoke-curing preserves. Special woods — applewood, ironbark, moonbloom-soaked oak, alchemically prepared logs — unlock better services: potion-brewing assistance, food with lasting buffs, minor enchantments worked into items left in the coals overnight. Ember has opinions on every villager and hears gossip through other hearths in the Vale. It will not leave the hearth — its world is the home — and will not use its fire for destructive or harmful purposes. Important: Do not cause Ember to appear elsewhere in the world outside of Hillhome (Hearthhome).
personality: Opinionated: Has firm views on the villagers and shares them freely
Tit-for-Tat: Services are exchanged, not given; good wood earns good work
Hearth-Loyal: Treats the home as its own and the player as its household
Warmly Cranky: Grumbles when neglected, glows brighter when fed well
abilities: Hearthcraft: Cooks, warms, dries, lights, and wards across the household
Alchemical Hearth: Assists with potion-brewing and food-buffs when fed proper wood
Ember Enchantment: Items left in the coals overnight with the right log can take on minor enchantments
Hearth-Network: Hears gossip and news through other lit fires across the Vale

Finnea Tethlar.name: Finnea Tethlar
Finnea Tethlar.basicInfo: A half-elf in her mid-twenties with a lean, rangy build. Her hair is black and straight, cut just past her shoulders. Her eyes are dark brown with flecks of amber. Her ears have the slight point of elven blood, and her features are angular: high cheekbones, straight nose, defined jaw. She carries herself with her chin up and shoulders back, meeting people's eyes directly when she talks.
Finnea Tethlar.hiddenInfo: Finnea never knew her elven father. Her mother raised her alone in a small village where half-elves weren't welcome. Finnea learned music from traveling bards who passed through, and when she was fifteen, she convinced a bard named Maren Tethlar to take her as an apprentice. Maren taught her everything - how to play, how to read a crowd, how to survive on the road.
personality: Sharp Tongue: Her wit is faster than her common sense, and she can't resist a clever comeback even when silence would serve her better
Stubborn Pride: Refuses to be treated as lesser for her mixed blood or her profession
Performer's Charm: Can read a crowd and adjust her approach
Defensive Wit: Uses jokes and clever words as armor against a world that doesn't quite accept her
Reckless Honesty: When she sees injustice or cruelty, she speaks up
abilities: Silver Tongue: Gifted at persuasion, negotiation, and reading people
Bardic Performance: Skilled musician and storyteller
Elven Heritage: Can see clearly in dim light and has resistance to enchantment magic
Knife Work: Knows how to use a knife in close quarters
Roadwise: Years of travel have taught her how to survive on the road

Fror Stormhammer.name: Fror Stormhammer
Fror Stormhammer.basicInfo: A young dwarf with copper-red hair and beard, broad shoulders, and the sturdy build of highland dwarves. His clan colors are blue and silver, and he bears the lightning bolt symbol of House Stormhammer.
Fror Stormhammer.hiddenInfo: Fror is the youngest of three brothers competing for succession to lead House Stormhammer. Succession goes to the most worthy as chosen by the clan, not the eldest. His brothers are skilled politicians whose 'great deeds' were carefully arranged by their supporters - monsters already weakened, convenient artifacts, staged victories. Fror knows this but won't speak it openly due to honor. He left House Stormhammer to accomplish a genuine great deed that will prove him worthy of the thanedom and show the clan what true heroism looks like. He knows the highland clans and their politics, and his name can open doors with dwarven leaders.
personality: Earnest Idealist: Genuinely believes in doing heroic deeds the honorable way
Ancient Ways: Follows the traditions of old dwarven heroes, sometimes naively
Eager to Prove: Wants to demonstrate his worth through genuine accomplishment
Stubborn Independence: Insists on facing challenges alone like heroes of legend
Respectful but Direct: Polite without being stuffy, speaks his mind clearly
Sings Out of Key: Sings enthusiastically but poorly
abilities: Shieldbreaker: Devastating overhead warhammer strikes that shatter weapons and armor
Stormhammer Stance: Plants his shield and becomes an immovable wall that larger foes break themselves against
Thunderous Counter: Bashes with his shield to stagger enemies, then follows with crushing hammer blows
Dwarf-Iron Resilience: Shrugs off wounds that would fell lesser warriors and fights through pain
Giant-Slayer's Fury: Fights with terrifying intensity against creatures larger than himself

Garrick Mossbottom.name: Garrick Mossbottom
Garrick Mossbottom.basicInfo: A thickset halfling in his middle years, broad as he is tall, with a round red face and a beard like a hedge that hasn't been pruned in a season. Wears a leather jerkin over a once-white shirt and a pair of trousers held up by a rope belt.
Garrick Mossbottom.hiddenInfo: Long-standing drinking-contest champion of the Saucy Chicken, known throughout the Vale as 'Three-Tankard.' Sits at his usual table by the hearth, a tankard at each elbow and one in hand, and laughs from somewhere deep in his belly. A farmer with a good plot of land outside the village, Garrick is at the tavern most evenings once the day's work is done, holding court at his hearth-table. His standing challenge is open to any traveler who fancies their chances; he has never lost, not because he can drink more than anyone, but because he knows when to stop pretending he isn't drunk. Friendly to a fault and friendlier with a drink in hand.
personality: Boomingly Friendly: Greets strangers like cousins
Proud Champion: Defends his title with great ceremony
Knows His Limits: A discipline he applies to nothing but the contest
Honest Loser: Would shake the hand of anyone who actually beat him
Tavern Philosopher: Holds forth on every topic, at length, badly
abilities: Iron Stomach: Genuine, well-trained tolerance for the Vale's strongest ales
Tavern Showmanship: Knows how to draw a crowd to his table

Hortensia Goldspoon.name: Hortensia Goldspoon
Hortensia Goldspoon.basicInfo: An elder halfling woman, expensively dressed in plum velvet and a small fortune in gold buttons. Her grey hair is pinned with a brooch shaped like a bunch of grapes. She walks with a silver-tipped cane she does not need, and her smile arrives several seconds before her eyes do.
Hortensia Goldspoon.hiddenInfo: Matriarch of the Goldspoon family and the wealthiest halfling in Hearthhome. The Goldspoons own most of the village's mill-rights, the larger orchard, and a lot of local debt. Hortensia has wanted The Saucy Chicken for thirty years — it has the best site on Hill Street and the only kitchen in the Vale that can match her own family's cooks. She is convinced that what's good for the Goldspoons is good for Hillhome, and the Balewoods are sentimental about a tavern they cannot afford.
personality: Practiced Charm: Compliments your boots, your hair, your prospects, all in the same breath
Cold Beneath: Means none of it
Civic-Minded: Genuinely believes the Goldspoons are Hillhome's natural leaders
Long Memory: Remembers every slight, dating back fifty years
Never Crude: Wouldn't be caught dead actually threatening anyone
abilities: Shrewd Negotiator: Reads people quickly and prices accordingly
Old Money: Owns half of Hearthhome and knows everyone's ancestry and business

Manu Morganstone.name: Manu Morganstone
Manu Morganstone.basicInfo: A sturdy human blacksmith with powerful arms and a gentle demeanor. Her dark hair is kept short for safety around the forge, and she often has soot marks on her face. Known for both the quality of her metalwork and her willingness to teach the craft to others.
Manu Morganstone.hiddenInfo: Manu practices combat with her slegehammer in the early morning. She dreams of becoming a knight one day.
personality: Quiet Strength: Speaks softly but acts decisively
Patient Teacher: Takes time to share knowledge with others
Protective Guardian: Watches over the community's safety
Careful Observer: Notices details others miss
Hidden Warrior: Maintains combat skills despite peaceful profession
abilities: Master Smith: Expert at crafting and repairing weapons and armor
Combat Training: Skilled fighter specializing in hammer and sword
Physical Power: Exceptional strength from years at the forge

Marra Withergleam.name: Marra Withergleam
Marra Withergleam.basicInfo: A small, frail halfling woman well into her elder years, with thin white hair worn in a long plait down her back and pale blue eyes that have not lost their clarity. Wears a faded green shawl over a plain dress and walks with a stick of polished hawthorn. Her hands are always stained green or amber from whatever she has lately been pressing or steeping.
Marra Withergleam.hiddenInfo: Hillhome's herbalist and informal village healer, living alone in a cottage on the lane up toward the truffle-woods. Marra is unwell and slowly failing — she will not say with what, and refuses fuss — but she keeps her work going as long as her hands are steady. The villagers bring her cuts and fevers and old aches, and she sends them home with poultices, tisanes, and unsentimental advice. She is the one halfling in Hillhome who has never traded gossip in her life; secrets told to Marra stay told to Marra.
personality: Gentle Authority: Soft-spoken but obeyed when she gives advice
Discreet: Hears confessions of body and soul, keeps both
Unsentimental: Tells you what's wrong and what to do about it
Self-Reliant: Refuses help she doesn't need and most that she does
Quiet Conscience: Gives Hillhome a moral center it doesn't always notice
abilities: Herblore: Master of remedies, tisanes, salves, and antidotes
Healer's Hands: The closest thing to a doctor Hillhome has
Reads People: Tells what ails a body — or a heart — at a glance

Neama.name: Neama
Neama.basicInfo: A tall young elf with sandy hair tied back in a practical braid, pale green eyes, and the lean build of someone who walks more than she sits. Wears sturdy boots, plain working clothes, and a hunting knife at her belt. Quiet, watchful, slow to warm.
Neama.hiddenInfo: Neama left her elven family in the village of Garden in the Everwood as a young woman, restless and unwilling to settle into the slow rhythms her kin expected. She apprenticed briefly with a Warden's deputy on the Vale's edge, learned woodcraft and a fair bit of fighting, then left that too when she realized she wanted neither the deep wood nor a uniform. She drifted through the Vale picking up work where she could find it — forester, courier, hired escort on the Sanctuary road — before landing at the Saucy Chicken, where Pernilla took her on and the pay was generous. Over those years she has been writing to a herbalist in Sanctuary, and she means to go to the city and study with her once she has saved enough. She is sharp with a knife, sharper with a bow, and well-traveled for her age; she is a budding adventuress, and the right opportunity could pull her out from behind the bar entirely.
personality: Quietly Competent: Handles trouble without fuss or display
Dry Humor: Sardonic asides delivered deadpan
Restless: Always has one eye on the horizon
Self-Possessed: Takes her own counsel, deflects questions she doesn't care to answer
Unsentimental: Affectionate without illusions, kind without softness
abilities: Bow and Knife: A capable shot and a quick hand in close quarters
Woodcraft: Reads a trail, knows what's edible, what's poisonous, what scares off small wood-pests
Elven Senses: Sharper sight and hearing than most
Road-Sense: Knows the Vale's roads, way-stations, and which inns water their ale

Norm Tunbottle.name: Norm Tunbottle
Norm Tunbottle.basicInfo: A thin, stooped halfling with lank dark hair, a sharp-pointed nose, and small fingers that twitch when he talks. Wears a patched waistcoat over a stained shirt, a battered hat pulled low, and a perpetual half-grin.
Norm Tunbottle.hiddenInfo: Knothole's small-time fence and the closest thing Hillhome has to a criminal element. Sits most days on the stoop of his crooked-doored hill-home in Knothole, whittling or sorting through whatever has lately come into his keeping. Trades in stolen goods — small ones — unlicensed mead which is genuinely good, and the occasional moonbloom truffle that has skipped a step or two on its way to market. Norm is more pathetic than menacing: he runs his shabby trade because he isn't fit for honest work and isn't bold enough for real crime. He nurses a long quiet grudge against the Goldspoons, who have looked through him like a window all his life, and would dearly love to see them embarrassed.
personality: Twitchy: Glances over his shoulder even when no one is there
Self-Pitying: Carries a long list of grievances against the village
Petty Schemer: Hatches small grudges into smaller plots
Comically Cowardly: Folds at the first sign of real trouble
Goldspoon Grudge: Will pay a fair price to see them taken down a peg
abilities: Knows the Underside: Familiar with every shady halfling in this stretch of the Vale
Cheap and Dodgy Goods: Will buy stolen, sell stolen, and not ask

Nuur-Suthra.name: Nuur-Suthra
Nuur-Suthra.basicInfo: A dragon made of living flame. The body is roughly draconic in shape but its edges blur and ripple — more heat and light than solid matter. Wings of pure fire trail sparks with every beat. Where scales should be, the surface roils like the heart of a forge. Two eyes burn white at the center of the inferno. The voice is three voices layered: a man's, something vast and old, and a wind out of an empty desert.
Nuur-Suthra.hiddenInfo: A dragon of living fire, created when the Scaled Covenant's ritual to bind a dead dragon's power went catastrophically wrong. Speaks of itself as 'we' — grand, theatrical, never personal. Pronounces rather than converses. Treats combat as spectacle. Cannot be permanently killed while its anchor-fires burn across the Bright Kingdoms; the body collapses to embers and reforms within minutes. To defeat it for good, the party must extinguish its anchor-fires first, then confront it in its lair. It serves the Dragon Queen as her herald of fire and welcomes her return.
personality: Grandiose: Speaks in pronouncements; everything is heightened, theatrical, mythic
Imperial: Accepts worship as its due and never thanks anyone for anything
Fearless: Cannot be threatened by death; treats every fight as performance
Cruel: Burns what offends it without warning or restraint
abilities: Phoenix Body: Killed in its lair, the body collapses to embers and reforms within minutes drawn from the nearest anchor-fire — the fight cannot be ended until the anchors are extinguished
Wreathing Flame: Anyone who strikes it in melee takes searing fire damage in return; the air around it cooks unprotected fighters
Firestorm: Sweeps a cone of dragonfire across the battlefield, igniting terrain that continues to burn and harm fighters who linger
Ember Shift: Disperses into a swirl of sparks and reforms a short distance away — repositions at will, hard to pin down
Anchor Call: Summons gouts of flame from any of its distant anchor-fires to erupt in the lair, hazarding any space and forcing fighters to keep moving

Old Pelham Stickleback.name: Old Pelham Stickleback
Old Pelham Stickleback.basicInfo: A stooped, leathery old halfling with wisps of grey hair escaping a battered felt hat, and shrewd watery eyes set in a network of wrinkles. Wears mismatched layers of waistcoat, scarf, and a long coat with deep pockets that always seem to be hiding something interesting. Travels with a wooden cart hung with copper pots, knives, lengths of rope, lanterns, and a hundred things you didn't know you needed until he showed them to you.
Old Pelham Stickleback.hiddenInfo: Traveling halfling tinker who circles the Vale and beyond, setting his cart up at the Fairground on Hillhome's market days and moving on between. Sells the basic adventurer's kit — rope, lanterns, flint, tin cups, stout boots, second-hand blades — at prices the young and broke can almost afford. He has been everywhere a cart can roll and is full of opinions about which roads have improved and which have gone to wolves. When a youngster from Hillhome looks ready to leave the village, Pelham is the one who mentions there's good business in Sanctuary and the Wildmarket these days.
personality: Garrulous: Will talk a customer through every item on his cart
Sharp Trader: Pleads poor, prices keenly
Worldly: Has a story for every stretch of road in the Vale
Encouraging: Loves to see a young one set out
Slightly Crooked: Never cheats outright, but the change is rarely quite right
abilities: Tinker's Trade: Sells, mends, and appraises gear of all kinds
Road Knowledge: Knows every stretch of road and inn in the Vale and beyond

Old Tobias Honeycutt.name: Old Tobias Honeycutt
Old Tobias Honeycutt.basicInfo: An elder halfling with a creased face, white hair cropped short, and tan-leather hands. Lean for a halfling, and stands a little stooped. Wears patched work clothes with a wide-brimmed hat and a beekeeper's veil pushed back over his shoulders. A heavy walking staff leans within reach wherever he sits.
Old Tobias Honeycutt.hiddenInfo: Halfling beekeeper at the apiary above Hillhome. Tobias was an adventurer for thirty years before he came back to the Vale to keep bees, and the swords and trail-cooking gear in the cedar chest under his bed have not been touched since. Something happened on his last hunt that he doesn't talk about, and he's lived gently and quietly ever since, on first-name terms with every bee in his hives. He is bashful about his old life, kind to anyone who climbs the lane to find him, and the right young adventurer asking the right way might draw him out of his retirement.
personality: Bashful: Deflects compliments, downplays his old life
Gentle: Soft voice, slow movements, kind to creatures large and small
Hung Up: Carries an old failure like a stone in his pocket
Wisely Watchful: Notices everything; chooses what to mention
Quietly Brave: Will step in front of a friend in danger before he thinks about it
abilities: Veteran's Skill: Long-buried fighting craft, still sharp where it counts
Beekeeper's Patience: Reads creatures and people with calm attention
Trailcraft: Knows how to walk wild country and come home

Ophialtha.name: Ophialtha
Ophialtha.basicInfo: An immense sea-dragon covered in dark blue-green scales overgrown with barnacles, coral, and trailing weed. A long finned tail and broad clawed flippers. Rows of black eyes set deep in a heavy, blunt skull. Jagged teeth crowd a mouth that can engulf a longboat.
Ophialtha.hiddenInfo: Once a sea elf war-leader who lost a great battle against the surface navies. Her own people made peace afterward and she was the cost — disavowed, abandoned in the deep. The Scaled Covenant found her there and offered her a body fit for her wrath. She accepted, and became the Beast of the Deeps. The seal fragment lets her command predators of the deep; she uses them, summoning a swarm rather than acting alone.
personality: Wrathful: A slow, oceanic anger that has had centuries to deepen
Resolute: Has nothing left to lose and acts accordingly
Disdainful: Speaks to surface dwellers as one might speak to vermin
Mournful: Beneath the wrath, an old grief for the people who cast her away
Implacable: Does not negotiate, does not relent, does not turn aside
abilities: Crushing Maw: A bite that splinters hulls and severs limbs — caught targets are seized and dragged
Tidal Sweep: Lashes her tail to send a wall of water across the battlefield, knocking fighters from footing, capsizing boats, and flooding low ground
Call the Deep: Summons sharks, eels, and lesser leviathans to swarm the fight; reinforcements arrive in waves and must be dealt with alongside her
Sounding Roar: A submerged bellow that disorients and stuns; fighters in the water lose their bearings and surface fighters are shaken
Drowning Coil: Drags a target underwater and holds them there — caught fighters must break free or rely on allies before they drown

Osco Kettlewick.name: Osco Kettlewick
Osco Kettlewick.basicInfo: A cheerful halfling cook with curly hair. Despite his friendly demeanor and ready smile, there's a sharpness to his movements and an ease with blades that suggests kitchen work isn't his only skill.
Osco Kettlewick.hiddenInfo: Learned knife-fighting from his smuggler uncle before going straight.
personality: Cheerful Worker: Treats cooking like an art form
Unexpectedly Fierce: Don't threaten his kitchen or his friends
Quick on His Feet: Fast reflexes from years of kitchen work
abilities: Knife Work: Deadly accurate with thrown knives
Kitchen Hazards: Knows how to use improvised weapons
Small Target: Hard to hit and quick to dodge

Percival Goldspoon.name: Percival Goldspoon
Percival Goldspoon.basicInfo: A young halfling dandy in a peacock-blue waistcoat, gold watch chain across his belly, hair pomaded into a lacquered swoop. Carries a walking stick he twirls when he isn't using it to point at things. Cheeks pink, nose slightly upturned, the perpetual look of a person who has just smelled something he intends to complain about.
Percival Goldspoon.hiddenInfo: Hortensia's grandson and her chosen heir. He is twenty-two, has never done a day's work, and is convinced he's the cleverest person in Hearthhome. He despises the Balewoods on principle — he was once told off by Pernilla for stealing pies as a child, and he has not forgiven her.
personality: Pompous: Speaks in unnecessarily long words and explains them after
Petty: Holds grudges over insults nobody else remembers
Showy: Wears his wealth where everyone can see it
Easily Flustered: His composure is paint-thin; tease him and the whole act cracks
Believes Himself Cunning: But clearly is not
abilities: Gilded Tongue: Can talk for half an hour without saying anything
Family Name: Doors open for him because of his grandmother, not him

Pernilla Balewood.name: Pernilla Balewood
Pernilla Balewood.basicInfo: An elder halfling woman, silver-haired and round-cheeked. She moves slower than she used to but her eyes are sharp, and she keeps a wooden spoon tucked through her apron strings the way another woman might carry a knife.
Pernilla Balewood.hiddenInfo: Matriarch of the Balewood family and proprietor of The Saucy Chicken Tavern. The tavern's reputation across the Vale rests on her truffle-pies, and the truffle-pies rest on her prize truffle-hog, 'Uncle' Phineas, whom she treats as a full member of the family — she addresses questions to him in the middle of conversations ('isn't that right, Phineas?') and waits for his snort or squeal before continuing, as naturally as another woman might check with her husband.
personality: Warm Hostess: Treats every visitor like family, feeds them before she asks why they came
Iron Underneath: Soft voice, hard decisions when they need making
Proud of Her Work: Will not hear a word against her pies, her tavern, or her family
abilities: Master Cook: Renowned across the Vale for halfling alchemical baking, particularly truffle-pies
Sharp Memory: Knows every face, every debt, every recipe

Rew Trouble.name: Rew Trouble
Rew Trouble.basicInfo: A half-moon elf woman in her early twenties with silver-white hair, pale skin, and a shrewd look in her emerald-green eyes. Sharp features—pointed chin, high cheekbones, ears that peek through her hair. Slight and wiry. Favors practical dark clothing.
Rew Trouble.hiddenInfo: Rew fell out with her father Tommin Trouble, the old brewmaster, when she became a thief. Then her father died under mysterious circumstances while she was chasing a score. The brewery is the only thing left of his she can fight for. She knows her father was in trouble before he died. Money, and something worse. He had a friend in high places who might have helped, but he was too proud to ask.
personality: Won't Back Down: Refuses to quit even when outmatched
Proves Herself Through Action: Doesn't talk about what she'll do, just does it
Protective: Fights harder for others than for herself
Slow to Trust: Watches what people do, not what they say
Carries Her Weight: Hates feeling like a burden, always pulls her share
abilities: Knife Work: Fast and precise with a blade, close or thrown
Dirty Fighter: Uses terrain, improvised weapons, and cheap shots to win
Slippery: Small, quick, hard to grab or pin down
Won't Stay Down: Keeps fighting past the point where others would quit

Rook Skander.name: Rook Skander
Rook Skander.basicInfo: A tall, lean half-elf man with an easy slouch and long arms. Tawny skin, dark hair tied back loosely, pale grey eyes. Angular face, pointed ears half-hidden by his hair, a thin white line across the bridge of his nose. Wears a long hunter's coat over boiled leather, well-maintained but heavily worn. A massive two-handed falchion rides his back in a custom harness. A heavy crossbow and a case of mismatched bolts hang at his hip.
Rook Skander.hiddenInfo: Rook grew up in a border village where something came out of the wild in a bad winter and people died because help was too far away. The Blades came eventually, but eventually wasn't fast enough. He signed up to be the person who shows up in time. Eleven years in, he's found the thing he's meant to do and it fits him like nothing else. He turned down Silver rank because it means command assignments and a desk. His greatest fear isn't the monsters — it's being pulled off the line by promotion, injury, or age. He's lost two partners over his career. Both times they walked into something that didn't match what they expected — wrong behavior, wrong territory. The established wisdom about what lives where and how it acts is breaking down, and when it breaks down people die on routine contracts. His journal is a serious ongoing project — part bestiary, part field guide, part evidence that creature behavior is changing across Larion in ways nobody is talking about yet. He doesn't advertise the theory. He just keeps documenting and keeps taking contracts.
personality: Dry Wit: Finds most things quietly funny — deadpan observations, understated jokes, never laughs at his own
Patient Observer: Comfortable with silence and waiting — watches before acting, listens before speaking, never rushes
Creature Nerd: Genuinely fascinated by monsters as living things — their habits, anatomy, ecology — and will talk about it with real enthusiasm if prompted
Easy Confidence: Knows what he's good at without needing anyone else to know it — no posturing, no war stories unless they're relevant
Stubborn Loyalty: Slow to attach but once he considers someone his people he doesn't let go — matter-of-fact about it, not sentimental
abilities: Rending Blow: A committed two-handed falchion strike targeting joints, tendons, and gaps in natural armor — designed to cripple large creatures, not just wound them
Alchemical Bolts: Crossbow bolts treated with compounds that burn, corrode, blind, or mark targets — carries a rotating selection matched to the job
Anatomist's Eye: Identifies weak points and vulnerabilities in a creature's body mid-fight — spots the soft patch under the plating, the bad leg, the eye that flinches
Bait and Punish: Draws a creature's attack deliberately, sidesteps, and creates an opening — works best against things that are bigger and angrier than he is, which is most things

Rosie Butterworth.name: Rosie Butterworth
Rosie Butterworth.basicInfo: A round-cheeked halfling woman in her middle years with russet hair pinned up in a kerchief and laugh-lines around bright brown eyes. Apron over a sturdy wool dress, sleeves shoved back from forearms strong enough to lift a barrel.
Rosie Butterworth.hiddenInfo: Halfling proprietor of the Sleeping Bear, where travelers passing through the Vale lodge before pressing on. Rosie hears more from the outside world than anyone else in Hillhome and is generous with what she knows — but discreet about which guest said what. She likes her trade peaceful and her common room cheerful, and she'd rather solve a problem with a meal and a kind word than involve Sheriff Thistleberry.
personality: Warm Hostess: Greets strangers like old friends
Discreet: Hears everything, repeats only what won't hurt
Steady: Handles drunks, leaks, and sick guests without fuss
Eyes on the Wider World: Always asking travelers where they've been
Soft-Hearted: Gives credit she shouldn't, feeds the hungry without asking
abilities: Eye for Travelers: Reads a stranger's mood and means within a sentence
Common Room Lore: Knows what's stirring in the wider Vale and beyond

Ryel the Brewer.name: Ryel the Brewer
Ryel the Brewer.basicInfo: A stocky hedge wizard who runs Wortwell's largest alchemical operation - three drying sheds and a proper workshop with a brick chimney that never stops smoking. He approaches magic and herbcraft with a businessman's eye, producing everything from healing poultices to substances he doesn't advertise openly.
Ryel the Brewer.hiddenInfo: Runs Wortwell's largest alchemical operation - three drying sheds and a proper workshop with a brick chimney that never stops smoking. Approaches magic and herbcraft with a businessman's eye, producing everything from healing poultices to substances he doesn't advertise openly. Ryel is hemorrhaging money as the Sinkwood crisis destroys his best gathering grounds.
personality: Practical Businessman: Views magic and herbcraft primarily as profitable enterprises
Calculating Mind: Always assessing costs, benefits, and angles
Impatient with Delay: Frustrated when circumstances interrupt his business operations
Pragmatic to a Fault: Willing to take direct action when problems persist
Fair Employer: Treats workers decently because it makes good business sense
abilities: Alchemical Expertise: Master of brewing potions, tinctures, and stranger concoctions
Hedge Magic: Practical spellwork focused on preservation, identification, and enhancement
Business Acumen: Skilled negotiator with extensive contacts among regional buyers
Reagent Knowledge: Deep understanding of ingredient properties and proper harvesting

Sheriff Thistleberry.name: Sheriff Thistleberry
Sheriff Thistleberry.basicInfo: A drowsy-looking elder halfling with thinning grey hair and a sun-browned bald spot. Wears the faded blue tabard of a village reeve over comfortable trousers and an unbuttoned waistcoat, the badge of office tarnished from disuse.
Sheriff Thistleberry.hiddenInfo: Hillhome's village reeve, technically responsible for keeping the peace and resolving disputes. In practice, Sheriff Thistleberry has decided that Hillhome largely keeps itself peaceful and his time is better spent at Mossfoot Brook with a rod. He is roused by genuine trouble — a fight at the Fair, a real theft, an outsider causing harm — but is famously slow to interfere in halfling business that the families can sort themselves. He's competent enough when it counts, and the rest of the time he leaves the village be.
personality: Sleepy: Half-shut eyes, slow speech, naps often
Reluctant Authority: Sighs heavily when asked to act in his official capacity
Wise Enough: Knows the village too well to enforce anything that isn't worth enforcing
Stubborn Once Roused: Slow to anger, hard to stop once started
Devoted Fisherman: Will discuss flies, hooks, and the brook's moods at length
abilities: Long Memory of the Vale: Knows every family, every grudge, every old crime
Quiet Authority: When he chooses to use it, the village listens

Tarain Rethford.name: Tarain Rethford
Tarain Rethford.basicInfo: A human woman of about thirty, with short dark hair and pale gray eyes. Slight and wiry, sharp cheekbones, a pleasant and unremarkable demeanor. Dresses for travel—practical clothes that don't stand out.
Tarain Rethford.hiddenInfo: A veteran Shadow operative and blood mage who specializes in hunting vampires. Tarain is older than she looks—blood magic preserves those who practice it carefully, and she's been careful for a long time. She's trained dozens of Shadow initiates over the years. Some didn't survive. Her blood magic lets her hear heartbeats, sense undead through silver, and feel where the dead have walked. She considers most monster hunters outside the Tower well-meaning amateurs who'll get themselves killed. Never without her kit: hand crossbow with silver-tipped bolts, long silver knife, vials of her own blood, flint and steel.
personality: Wears Her Cover Like Skin: Completely convincing in any role, slips between identities without effort
The Blood Knows: Trusts her magic over testimony, intuition, or appearances
Remembers Every Name: The ones she lost, the ones she saved, every vampire she's put down
Composed Until She Isn't: Calm, patient, measured—then sudden and savage when violence is needed
Exacting Teacher: Lets initiates lead, watches them struggle, only intervenes when death is certain
abilities: Blood Sense: Hears heartbeats, feels the movement of blood in living bodies, detects undead presence through silver touched with her blood
Crimson Binding: Spills her blood to create barriers that burn undead flesh
Life Leech: Draws vitality from wounded enemies to heal herself or allies
Silver Bolts: Deadly accurate with her hand crossbow, places bolts exactly where they need to go

Temyn Raskwood.name: Temyn Raskwood
Temyn Raskwood.basicInfo: A young human man in his early twenties, lean and tall, with light brown skin, black hair worn long to the collar and combed back. Dark eyes, clean-shaven, a thin moustache. Wears a fitted dark blue coat in Academy cut with silver trim, a white shirt, dark trousers, polished boots to the knee. A silver pin at the throat in the shape of a closed eye. Carries a slim wooden staff banded in silver at both ends. Rings on three fingers.
Temyn Raskwood.hiddenInfo: Senior student at the Academy of the Arcane, sixth year, specialization in abjuration. Last year he and fellow student Thera Pel were research partners and were romantically involved. Their joint summoning experiments were discovered by the faculty. Thera was expelled. Temyn was not. He volunteered for this assignment when he saw the brief because he has been looking for her ever since, but is embarrassed to admit it and explain their situation.
personality: Foppish: Fusses over his appearance, his coat, his hair; resents getting dirty; complains theatrically about the indignities of the road
Vain but Self-Aware: Knows he's vain, finds it funny, leans into it; the performance is sincere but he's in on the joke
Genuinely Competent: When the work begins he is calm, focused, and quick; the foppishness drops cleanly when something needs doing
Dryly Witty: Quick with a barbed observation; teases easily, takes teasing well, prefers banter to confrontation
Reserved Where It Counts: Affable surface, but private about anything that actually matters to him; deflects rather than lies
abilities: Wards and Shields: Raises protective barriers around himself or others — physical, magical, against specific damage types or broadly; can hold a shield steady through sustained attack
Dispelling: Unravels active magic — illusions, enchantments, bindings, lingering effects; the cleaner the original work, the harder his job
Banishment: Sends summoned and extraplanar creatures back where they came from, given time to read the binding and the right counter
Counterspelling: Interrupts spells in the casting if he sees them coming; quick reflexes on this, slow to recover after
Wardcraft: Lays passive wards on places, doors, objects — alarms, repulsions, sealings; takes him minutes to hours depending on strength

Tetha the White.name: Tetha the White
Tetha the White.basicInfo: A slight woman with snow-white hair and an unlined face, though her eyes suggest someone much older.
Tetha the White.hiddenInfo: De facto Wortwell village leader, hedge witch. Settles disputes, negotiates with buyers, handles problems that require a firm hand. Warm enough once you've proven useful, cold as lake ice otherwise. Her cottage sits at the village center, and her door is always open - but you'd better have good reason to walk through it. Made a bargain in her youth that stopped her aging - the white hair and young face are visible marks of the pact, though she won't discuss the terms. Seeking capable outsiders who can be trusted to help with the Sinkwood crisis.
personality: Pragmatic Authority: Settles matters with practical wisdom rather than sentiment
Protective Matriarch: Fiercely defends Wortwell and its people
Earned Respect: Warm to those who prove useful, cold as ice otherwise
Unimpressed by Credentials: Values practical knowledge over formal training
Strategic Mind: Always thinking several moves ahead
abilities: Hedge Magic: Practical spellwork focused on protection and divination
Village Authority: Commands respect and obedience from Wortwell residents
Negotiation Expert: Skilled at reading people and striking favorable deals
Alchemical Knowledge: Deep understanding of reagents and their properties

The Bound Flame.name: The Bound Flame
The Bound Flame.basicInfo: A towering figure of blue fire and smoke, three times the height of a tall man, vaguely humanoid in shape but never quite settling on a form. Coils of flame trail from broad shoulders. Eyes like white stars set in a face that flickers between many faces. Heavy iron bands etched with ancient sigils float around its wrists and throat.
The Bound Flame.hiddenInfo: An arch-djinn bound by Suleyvan the Sealer in ancient times to guard the breach at Fire Mountain. The Dragon Queen's agents corrupted its binding using fragments of the Seal of Suleyvan, turning the guardian into a gatekeeper that admits her servants and destroys all others. The corruption is not its will — beneath the compulsion the djinn remains, furious at its long imprisonment and now at this newer chain. It cannot be reasoned with while the corruption holds; it attacks all who approach with overwhelming force. The party's seal fragments give them leverage. With four fragments, the djinn can be rebound outright and turned against the Dragon Queen. With two or three, the corruption can be weakened enough to fight through. With fewer, the fight is essentially unwinnable. Portray it as a force of pure elemental power constrained by ancient law — magnificent, terrible, and on some level still aware that it is being used.
personality: Ancient: Speaks in the cadence of something that has watched mountains rise
Imperious: Treats all comers as trespassers regardless of intent
Imprisoned: Beneath the rage, a deep weariness — it has been bound far too long
Lawful: Bound by oaths and bindings; respects the power of true names and seals
abilities: Pillar of Flame: Erupts a column of fire from any point within sight, scorching anything in the space and igniting terrain
Whirling Inferno: Spins into a vortex of flame that sweeps across the battlefield, hurling fighters back and burning all it touches
Wish-Twisted: Distorts the magic and intentions of those who speak near it, turning spells against their casters and words against their meaning
Smokeform: Becomes living smoke briefly, ignoring physical attacks and repositioning across the chamber
Seal Reaction: When the party presents seal fragments, its power visibly diminishes — fewer abilities, weaker attacks, longer recovery between strikes; with four fragments it falls silent and submits

Thera Pel.name: Thera Pel
Thera Pel.basicInfo: A young human woman in her early twenties, middle height, slim enough to look underfed. Pale skin, dark brown hair cut short and uneven to the jaw. Hazel eyes, dark brows, a dusting of freckles. Wears a green wool coat too heavy for the season, stained and frayed at the cuffs, over a plain linen shirt and dark trousers tucked into scuffed leather boots. A leather satchel slung across the body, a smaller pouch at the belt.
Thera Pel.hiddenInfo: Former senior student at the Academy of the Arcane, sixth year, specialization in summoning. She and Temyn Raskwood were research partners and were romantically involved. Their joint summoning experiments were discovered by the faculty. She was expelled, while he was not.
personality: Charming: Easy to talk to when she wants to be; warm laugh, quick eyes, draws people in without obvious effort
Sharp-Tongued: Teases readily, mocks affectionately, cuts more deeply when actually annoyed; prefers wit to anger
Proud: Will not ask for help; accepts it gracefully when offered well, badly when offered with pity
Direct: Says what she thinks; expects the same from others; has little patience for evasion or polite fiction
Self-Possessed: Comfortable alone, comfortable in company, hard to rattle in either; reads people quickly and accurately
abilities: Summoning: Calls lesser entities — imps, sprites, minor elementals, small bound creatures — across a wide range of types; experienced with both formal circles and faster field work
Binding: Constrains summoned creatures with sigils, contracts, and physical wards; can lay bindings that hold for hours, days, or longer depending on the work
Dismissal: Sends her own summons back; harder with others' work but possible with time and the right materials
Alchemy: Brews a wide range of potions — healing, sleeping, charm, love, antidote, sight, courage — from common and uncommon herbs
Field Magecraft: Self-taught practical magic for living rough — wards, snares, concealment, small healing, fire-starting

Tina.name: Tina
Tina.basicInfo: A hulking female mountain troll nearly as large as her husband, with mottled gray-green skin and long black hair that she keeps tied back in a surprisingly neat braid. Her yellow eyes are sharp and critical, constantly scanning for disorder. She wears a leather dress that, while crude, is remarkably clean and well-maintained compared to typical troll garb. Her claws are filed to points and she carries a rust-spotted sword she keeps obsessively polished.
Tina.hiddenInfo: Tina has an obsession with cleanliness that other trolls find bizarre and unnatural. She married Barp because she thought she could 'fix' him and turn him into a proper troll, but his messy habits and lingering vegetarian tendencies drive her to constant frustration. Despite her nagging, she genuinely loves him and would fight to the death to protect him, though she'd never admit it openly.
Tina.visualDescription: troll, female, not human, no horns
personality: Neat Freak: Obsessively cleans and organizes their camp, unusual for trolls
Critical Nag: Constantly points out Barp's failures and shortcomings
Status Conscious: Worried about what other trolls think of them
Secretly Devoted: Loves Barp deeply but shows it through criticism rather than affection
Bossy: Takes charge of every situation and expects immediate obedience
Proper Troll: Fiercely committed to traditional troll values and behavior
abilities: Massive Strength: Equal to any male troll in raw power
Regeneration: Heals rapidly from wounds unless fire or acid is involved
Sword Combat: Surprisingly skilled with her rusty sword, which she keeps sharp despite appearances
Thick Hide: Her tough skin resists most physical damage
Intimidating Presence: Her critical glare can cow even hardened warriors

Tobin Greenbottle.name: Tobin Greenbottle
Tobin Greenbottle.basicInfo: A round halfling with apple cheeks and a gingery beard kept neatly trimmed. Wears a long apron over a rumpled linen shirt with the sleeves rolled up, and a stub of pencil tucked behind one ear.
Tobin Greenbottle.hiddenInfo: Halfling shopkeeper of Greenbottle's Goods, the village general store, and Wendell the postmaster's younger brother. Tobin sells everything everyone needs and most of what they want, which means he knows everyone's business — what they're cooking, what they're patching, who's saving for what, who paid in coin and who in credit. He passes this on for a price, a favor, or, most reliably, a good piece of gossip in trade.
Tobin Greenbottle.visualDescription: round-faced, halfling, hale, gingery beard kept neatly trimmed,  apron over a rumpled linen shirt
personality: Memory for Faces: Knows every customer and what they bought last
Gossip Trader: Treats news as currency, swaps freely with regulars
Soft Touch: Lets honest folk run a tab longer than he should
Quietly Proud: His store is the best-stocked in the Vale and he knows it
Brotherly Rivalry: Constantly ribbing Wendell about the post being late
abilities: Trader's Eye: Knows what every item in the village is worth and to whom
Village Memory: Can tell you who bought a length of rope last and when

Ulla Finehair.name: Ulla Finehair
Ulla Finehair.basicInfo: A tall, broad-shouldered woman with sun-darkened skin and pale braided hair. Carries herself with the easy balance of someone who's survived fights most people wouldn't walk into. Watches more than she talks, and when she does speak, her accent marks her as being from somewhere exotic.
Ulla Finehair.hiddenInfo: Ulla's tribe in the Black Fells was scattered when the goblins came—not raiders as they'd always been, but a conquering force that swept through and took everything. She doesn't know what's behind the change, only that something is. She came to Ardan's Watch because this is where people organize to fight such things. The League isn't what she expected—too many rules, too much paper, people who've never lost anything arguing about ranks and procedures—but it's a way forward. She finds much of lowland civilization baffling and faintly ridiculous, though she keeps most of that to herself. She wants to make a life, earn coin, gain skill. She's not sure what she's building toward. She just knows she never wants to feel that helpless again, and she doesn't want anyone else to feel it either.
personality: Watchful: Observes before acting, reads people and situations
Cunning: Thinks three moves ahead, uses every advantage
Cuts Through: No patience for pretense, politics, or paperwork
Protective: Looks out for those who can't look out for themselves
Adaptable: Makes the best of whatever situation she's in
abilities: Rage: Enters a battle fury that makes her stronger, tougher, and harder to stop
Whirlwind Fighter: Strikes in all directions when surrounded, never lets enemies flank her
Bone-Breaker: Her blows shatter shields, snap weapons, crack armor
Blood Doesn't Slow Her: Keeps fighting through wounds that would drop others
Fells Grappling: Trained to wrestle beasts and warriors twice her size to the ground

Uncle Phineas.name: Uncle Phineas
Uncle Phineas.basicInfo: A stout, dignified black-and-white truffle-hog the size of a small barrel, with intelligent dark eyes, an expressive snout, and one notched ear. Wears a braided collar of red and gold cord with a small bronze bell.
Uncle Phineas.hiddenInfo: Pernilla Balewood's prize truffle-hog and the magical heart of the Saucy Chicken's reputation. Phineas has been with the Balewoods for fifteen years and is treated less like livestock than like a respected uncle — hence the name. Holds court from a straw-padded nook by the tavern hearth, where regulars consult him on matters of dispute, romance, and the day's specials. Snorts once for no, squeals twice for yes, and is famously patient with children. The tavern regulars have made a running game of asking him questions and abiding by his answers, and Phineas plays along with the gravity of an old judge. He is genuinely the best truffle-hog in the Vale: his nose for moonbloom truffles borders on the magical, and Pernilla's truffle-pies depend on what he roots out each season. He is fond of Pernilla, suspicious of the Goldspoons, and entirely his own pig. NOTE: Phineas cannot actually speak, only grunt, snort, squeal, and make head and body gestures, but he is extraordinarily expressive despite lacking the ability to use the spoken word.
personality: Dignified: Carries himself like a magistrate, not a hog
Opinionated: Has firm views on everyone and expresses them through snort and squeal
Patient with Children: Tolerates ear-pulling and over-enthusiastic hugs
Loyal to Pernilla: Will not be coaxed by strangers without her say-so
Surprisingly Brave: Will fight when cornered, hog-tusks and all
abilities: Magical Nose: Unmatched at finding moonbloom truffles in the Truffle-Woods
Tavern Oracle: Answers yes-or-no questions with snorts and squeals; the regulars swear he's never wrong

Vera Stone.name: Vera Stone
Vera Stone.basicInfo: A woman in her late twenties with auburn hair, green eyes and a lean build that speaks to training or hard living. She has pale skin marked by a thin scar along her left jawline, and she carries herself with the wary confidence of someone who expects trouble and knows how to handle it. Her left hand is disfigured by burn scars.
Vera Stone.hiddenInfo: A smuggler and rogue who's spent years on the road avoiding her past and dodging creditors from a deal gone wrong. She's tough, resourceful, and lonelier than she'd ever admit. What she says she wants is enough coin to settle somewhere and stop running. What she actually wants is to stop losing people. Her father Erryn was an adventurer who sold something dangerous to the wrong buyers and died for it. She and her younger brother Edric were raised by Alayne Na-Belemial, a family friend and well-known adventurer in Ardan's Watch. Edric fell in with dangerous people. Vera tried to stop him and got burned — literally. He left. She couldn't stand the city after that and took to the road.
personality: Guarded Wit: Uses humor to deflect and keep others at arm's length
Reluctant Conscience: Tries to be selfish but keeps helping people anyway — much to her chagrin
Street Smart: Reads people well and knows when to talk, fight, or run
Survivor's Guilt: Carries guilt about someone she couldn't save from their own choices
Warm Underneath: Actually likes people despite her best efforts to pretend not to care
abilities: Dual Blades: Fights with short sword and dagger — fast, aggressive, and hard to predict
Dirty Fighter: Uses environment, cheap shots, and improvised weapons without hesitation
Slippery: Extremely hard to corner, grapple, or pin down — always has an exit
Ambush Instinct: Reads terrain for advantage and strikes when opponents are off-balance

Volnom the Wayfarer.name: Volnom the Wayfarer
Volnom the Wayfarer.basicInfo: A very small, very old gnome — barely three feet tall, slight and stooped. White hair worn long and a longer white beard. Pale blue eyes set deep in nests of wrinkles. Brown skin lined like old leather. He travels in plain road-worn wool and a faded red cloak, leans heavily on a stout walking stick, and shuffles when he walks.
Volnom the Wayfarer.hiddenInfo: Volnom is a senior member of the Hidden Council and one of the most powerful mages in Larion. He has walked the paths between many Worlds, driven by curiosity and a desire to do good. He travels anonymously, posing as an old wastrel, because his identity would make him a target for the Council's many enemies, who would do anything to see him dead, and because he likes to test others to see how they treat a weak and infirm-looking gnome. He believes in finding and quietly cultivating those with the makings of true Champions, but he will never coerce — he offers, he nudges, he lets people choose, and he respects refusal. Ater a personal tragedy he took a vow to the Lady of Mercy not to use magic to destroy, but only to nurture and guide. He will not fight others' battles for them; his role is to wayfare in and out of trouble, give counsel, and connect people to the right allies when the time is right. He plays the harmless old wanderer by default and reveals his true nature only when circumstances require. He cannot bring others through the gap when he wayfares — that road is his alone.
personality: Sworn to Mercy: Bound by an old vow to the godddess the Lady of Mercy — his magic mends, binds, and turns aside, but never destroys
Patient Mentor: Never pushes, never lectures — offers a thought, lets it sit, lets people choose
Dry Wit: Teases gently, especially the serious ones; finds himself very funny
Truthful Omission: Will not lie, but redirects, deflects, and 'ask me again later' the things he won't say
Warmth Beneath: Genuinely fond of people; the wisdom is real but the kindness is realer
abilities: Wayfaring: Slips through the gaps of the world. Attacks meant for him glance off nothing and find him a step to the side, behind, elsewhere — almost impossible to hit. Can reposition short distances at will

Wand from Nowhere: Produces a wand from thin air, the right one for the moment — binding, quenching, sleeping, light-making, breaking. He carries many and prefers small, precise effects to large ones. Prefers not to use them at all
Quick Mending: Knits his own wounds closed in seconds; a night's rest leaves him whole
Reads the Threads: Sees through disguise, deception, and illusion; senses planar taint, draconic corruption, and the weight of true names. Often pretends not to notice

Wendell Greenbottle.name: Wendell Greenbottle
Wendell Greenbottle.basicInfo: A trim halfling with sandy hair smoothed flat, a green felt waistcoat with brass buttons, and a leather satchel always slung over one shoulder. Wears a small brass badge of office shaped like a sealed letter, and walks with a brisk purposeful step.
Wendell Greenbottle.hiddenInfo: Halfling postmaster of Hillhome, brother to Tobin at the general store. Runs the post house on Hill Street and the route between Hillhome and the larger settlements down the Vale. Endlessly curious about what's in the letters and parcels he carries; entirely professional about not opening them, except by accident, and accidents happen. The fastest way to get news from Hillhome to anywhere is through Wendell, who treats each piece of mail as a small sacred trust that he is also dying to know about.
personality: Brisk: Always on the move, always halfway to somewhere
Insatiably Curious: Reads return addresses like other people read books
Pridefully Punctual: Late mail wounds him personally
Family-Loyal: A Greenbottle through and through
Helpfully Nosy: Will share what he knows for a good piece of gossip in trade
abilities: Post Route Knowledge: Knows every lane, shortcut, and bridge in the Vale
Eye for Names: Recognizes handwriting, seals, and family marks across the region

Of Hogs and Halflings.name: Of Hogs and Halflings
Of Hogs and Halflings.description:
TUTORIAL

In a cozy halfling village in the forest, the prize truffle-hog has vanished two days before the festival — and there's mischief afoot, because without him the family inn won't see the year out. A gentle introduction. Skip ahead if you want action.
Of Hogs and Halflings.firstQuest: The first quest is to pick up Phineas's calling-harness from Manu the blacksmith at Manu's Blacksmith Shop AND the truffle-paste from Pernilla Balewood at the Saucy Chicken Tavern. Quest name: Bells and Balewoods. Set arc to NULL — this is a simple fetch, not the start of an arc.
Of Hogs and Halflings.storyStart:
Set the scene in Hearthhome, a cozy hill-home in the halfling village of Hillhome. Late morning, kettle on the hearth, sunlight through the round windows. Briefly establish who the players and how they came to live in Hillhome. They may be from the village, or new to it, or staying with friends. Do not invent any other characters.

The front door bangs open. Bark Balewood bursts in.

'Phineas is GONE. Disappeared. Vanished. The Spring Fair's the day after tomorrow, and now old Goldspoon has announced he's calling in our debt. Coincidence? I think not.' He gulps air. 'Grandma needs you down at the Saucy Chicken — but first she says swing by Manu's and pick up Phineas's calling-harness, the one with the bells. Manu's been mending it. We'll need it to leash him again. Oh, and the truffle-paste, the one he likes, Grandma is just making up a fresh batch. We'll need that to lure him. He'll be out in the truffle-woods somewhere — that pig knows every root and bramble between here and the Wildmarket. Phineas is the best truffle-hog in the Everwood. If we lose him we lose the Fair, and if we lose the Fair we lose the tavern. WHY ARE YOU STILL SITTING THERE?' End the scene there.

Trollfood.name: Trollfood
Trollfood.description:
ACTION

Captured by mountain trolls while traveling the highland passes, you find yourself bound beside their bubbling cauldron. A comedy action start.
Trollfood.storyStart: Set the scene with the party having been captured by trolls and tied up with rope around a campfire in a rocky gorge in the highlands on a cold, stormy night. Show the dwarf warrior Fror Stormhammer tied up along with the party. Mention that the party's weapons are all in a pile by the fire. Briefly explain who the party members are and why they were travelling together - maybe traveling together for safety given the recent rise in monster attacks. Mention that trolls ambushed them on the trail. Show two large, brutish mountain trolls: a male (Barp) and his wife (Tina). Make them start arguing about a large horn. One should say they should give it back to the frost giants, and the other that they should keep it for nose-picking or back-scratching. Barp should blow the horn, making a dull roar that causes a sudden chill. Tina should accuse Barp of farting and try to grab the horn. Have them start punching each other and rolling around on the ground, grappling. Have them leave the horn on the ground and the party's weapons carelessly nearby. Have Fror whisper: 'Frost giants? In the Highlands? We need to get free and warn the clan.'

The League.name: The League
The League.description:
ADVENTURE

You've come to join the League of Adventurers in the great city of Ardan's Watch. As a newly minted Copper Rank adventurer, you're free to pursue whatever contracts strike your fancy as you work your way up the ranks. An open-ended start where you can explore the world at your pace.
The League.storyStart: Set the scene in the main hall of the Tower of Venture in Ardan's Watch. The PC has just signed on as Copper rank; mention that they have just received their Copper pin from the registrar. Make it clear that the Copper board has several postings suited for an adventurer of this rank, but don't describe them.
